Operating Model

Home → Demo Hub → Operating Model

Request flow

A future request would move through a controlled readiness chain.

This is the conceptual operating model for how the command center would handle a request if runtime activation were ever separately approved. The current build stops at readiness and review. In this demo, registered agents are command-center operating units from the static org-chart registry, not live autonomous processes.

Important note

This is the readiness flow, not an enabled execution flow.

Input / Request

A request enters the system as a reviewable unit.

→

Identity / Auth Boundary

Access control and identity checks would happen here.

→

Storage / Request Record

The request would be stored as a record before any other step.

→

Audit Ledger

Every important action would be recorded for traceability.

→

Approval Gate

Human approval would be required before moving on.

→

Dry-Run Preview

The effect would be simulated without real-world mutation.

→

Controlled Action Queue

Requests would be ordered, not executed.

→

Human Approval / Attestation

A second human review gate would keep the process controlled.

→

Execution Readiness Check

The system would confirm prerequisites before any runtime.

→

Monitoring / Rollback / Incident Readiness

Operational oversight would need to be ready before live action.

→

Human Review / Go-No-Go

Final decision stays with humans, not the dashboard.

11 operational domains

Demo-facing operating domain map

Source type: demo-facing operating domain map derived from MVP-43 through MVP-50 readiness architecture.

  • Authentication / identity readiness
  • Request storage readiness
  • Audit ledger readiness
  • Approval gate readiness
  • Dry-run readiness
  • Controlled action queue readiness
  • Human-approved execution readiness
  • Monitoring / rollback / incident readiness
  • Demo / stakeholder review readiness
  • Validator / quality gate readiness
  • Safety boundary readiness

Where auth fits

At the front of the flow, before storage or any downstream handling.

Where audit fits

Across the entire flow so the chain is explainable and reviewable.

Where runtime stays separate

Runtime activation is a future phase with its own approval and security requirements.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a static, read-only demonstration. It does not enable live automation, command execution, database writes, Supabase mutations, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or runtime activation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
